title: 1981-04-16_Pat_62.txt
key_claims: Dick asserts that his *Valis* trilogy (*VALIS*, *The Divine Invasion*, *The Transmigration of Timothy Archer*) is a complete study of what Christ is and how he is generated. Christ (Sophia/Angel Archer) is the character common to all three.

He clarifies that he *did not become Christ* in 1974. Rather, he and the adventitious psyche were combined like base metals in an *alchemical transmutation* (the Mysterium Coniunctionis), and the *resulting compound* was Christ (Ditheon).
He establishes a strict hierarchy of 3 Ontological Levels corresponding to Dante's *Commedia*:
Freedom consists of the human (the true self) triumphing over Schopenhauer's blind, impersonal "Will," which formerly controlled the empirical ego.
recurring_concepts: **Spiritual Alchemy**: The realization that Hermetic alchemy was a coded method for combining mundane psychic elements (left and right hemispheres) to produce a divine entity (Gold/Christ).
**Christogenesis**: The ongoing evolutionary process of building God out of human parts.
reading_excerpt: Dick asserts that his *Valis* trilogy (*VALIS*, *The Divine Invasion*, *The Transmigration of Timothy Archer*) is a complete study of what Christ is and how he is generated. Christ (Sophia/Angel Archer) is the character common to all three.
